Title: Synthesis of flavour-related linalool is regulated by PpbHLH1 and associated with changes in DNA methylation during peach fruit ripening

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Prunus persica; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hujingmilu; Col-0 tps10 mutant background; MicroTom
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit flavour/aroma; consumer preference; also discussed as defense-related volatile and pollinator attractant
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ripe peach fruit; transgenic tomato fruit; Arabidopsis whole plants; tomato leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-g family terpene synthase; PF01397 and PF03936 domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GPP to linalool in vitro, with ~98.3% of products as linalool and ~95% of that linalool as S-(+)-linalool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes linalool accumulation; overexpression increased peach linalool 3.06-fold and tomato fruit linalool ~4.4-fold, while VIGS reduced peach linalool by ~66.5%
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; chiral GC-MS; transient overexpression in peach fruit; VIGS in peach fruit; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis tps10 mutant complementation; stable overexpression in tomato; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases during peach fruit development/ripening and positively correlates with linalool content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: in transgenic Arabidopsis and tomato, ectopic expression caused higher linalool accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGreen-SK transient overexpression in peach; pBI121 for Arabidopsis stable expression; pBI121 for tomato stable expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening; UV-B irradiation reduced its expression and linalool accumulation in peach
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA576753 and SRP103523; whole-genome bisulfite sequencing reported but accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: decreased promoter DNA methylation during peach fruit ripening was associated with increased PpTPS3 expression; no remarkable methylation increase after UV-B despite reduced transcript
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable metabolic engineering target to enhance fruit linalool/flavour; effective in peach transient assays and heterologous Arabidopsis/tomato systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: high linalool may have cytotoxic effects as discussed; some produced linalool can be glycosylated or oxidized, indicating metabolic diversion/detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpTPS3 expression strongly correlated with linalool accumulation during peach ripening and across cultivars. 2. Recombinant PpTPS3 converted GPP to linalool, mainly S-(+)-linalool. 3. Overexpression increased and silencing decreased peach fruit linalool, confirming in vivo function. 4. Heterologous expression elevated linalool in Arabidopsis and tomato. 5. Reduced promoter DNA methylation during ripening was associated with increased PpTPS3 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a novel peach TPS associated particularly with S-(+)-linalool formation; more specific for linalool than previously reported PpTPS1; no transit peptide detected despite monoterpene synthase activity.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Prunus persica; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit flavour/aroma; consumer preference; also discussed as defense-related volatile and pollinator attractant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ripe peach fruit; tobacco leaves; Arabidopsis plants; tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of linalool via transcriptional activation of linalool synthase gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpTPS3; heterologous targets NtLIS; AtTPS10; SlTPS5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PpTPS3 by binding an E-box (CACATG) in its promoter; overexpression also upregulated NtLIS, AtTPS10 and SlTPS5 in heterologous systems
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive activator of peach linalool biosynthesis identified from 58 TFs, showing the strongest induction of the PpTPS3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: fruit ripening; UV-B light negatively affects its expression; promoter enriched for light-responsive cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes linalool accumulation; overexpression increased peach linalool and mainly S-(+)-linalool, while silencing reduced PpTPS3 transcript by ~53% and linalool by ~54.4%
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-luciferase screening; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; subcellular localization; transient overexpression in peach fruit and tobacco; VIGS in peach fruit; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis and tomato; metabolite profiling; correlation across cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed during peach fruit ripening; positively correlated with PpTPS3 and linalool across cultivars; reduced after UV-B treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ectopic overexpression in tobacco, Arabidopsis and tomato increased endogenous linalool synthase gene expression and linalool accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGreen-SK transient overexpression in peach and tobacco; pBI121 in Arabidopsis; pBIN19-E8 in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening; UV-B irradiation suppressed PpbHLH1 expression and linalool synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA576753 and SRP103523; whole-genome bisulfite sequencing reported but accession not provided
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: no inverse association between DNA methylation and PpbHLH1 expression during peach ripening; UV-B reduced transcript without remarkable methylation change
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate regulator for engineering fruit flavour by boosting linalool; demonstrated cross-species utility in tobacco, Arabidopsis and tomato
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: elevated linalool may require downstream glycosylation/oxidation detoxification; sensory acceptance of engineered tomato not tested
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpbHLH1 was the strongest activator of the PpTPS3 promoter among 58 tested TFs. 2. It directly bound the proximal E-box in the PpTPS3 promoter and activated transcription. 3. Its expression positively correlated with PpTPS3 and linalool levels across peach cultivars. 4. Overexpression increased, and silencing decreased, peach fruit linalool accumulation. 5. Heterologous expression elevated endogenous linalool synthase genes and linalool in tobacco, Arabidopsis and tomato. 6. UV-B repressed PpbHLH1 expression, linking environmental control to linalool biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not activate PpTPS1 promoter in this study; paper notes other TFs such as PpNAC1, PpNAC2 and PpERF61 also showed activation effects in screening but were not the main experimentally validated regulators.
